author: @BenisJam
text: That's wild. I thought the advertising looked neat. I like the style, the look, the design of the Bride and I like Christian Bale. But soon as they said it was feminist and directed by a woman, I was absolutely disappointed. Much like the Barbie movie, which caught a lot of us from its fun advertising and look, seemingly like a movie everyone could enjoy, but then turning out to be a disgusting, hateful piece of work, that was awful. And since Barbie was a more popular movie, everyone caught the disappointment. My mom, my little sister, my girlfriend... all hated Barbie. The Bride looks like more of the same and it's a shame because give the set/costume designer and cinematropher to any other filmmaker (one with no intention to proselytize), and this could've been a sleeper hit.
